title: empty file when exporting
body:
Version is podman-0.12.1.2-1.git9551f6b.fc29.x86_64 from F29 updates-testing.
I am running podman as user, not root.

$ podman container ls
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                          COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS  NAMES
1ffe1ad85dee  localhost/teamviewer10:latest  /bin/sh -c /opt/t...  11 seconds ago  Up 11 seconds ago         vibrant_stallman

$ podman export 1ffe1ad85dee -o /tmp/tv.tar

File /tmp/tv.tar is 1024 bytes long and contains 0x00 only.

comments.body:
@giuseppe Another fuse-overlayfs not leaving the user namespace issue?

On Wed, Dec 19, 2018, 07:35 Nerijus Baliūnas <notifications@github.com
wrote:

> Version is podman-0.12.1.2-1.git9551f6b.fc29.x86_64 from F29
> updates-testing.
> I am running podman as user, not root.
>
> $ podman container ls
> CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES
> 1ffe1ad85dee localhost/teamviewer10:latest /bin/sh -c /opt/t... 11 seconds
> ago Up 11 seconds ago vibrant_stallman
>
> $ podman export 1ffe1ad85dee -o /tmp/tv.tgz
>
> File /tmp/tv.tgz is 1024 bytes long and contains 0x00 only.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2027>, or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCIOz0RqYarI1XRtU19573uEqzTYLks5u6jKsgaJpZM4ZaKD3>
> .
>

Thank you, exporting works now. But when importing on another PC I get an error:

$ podman import /tmp/tv.tar 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:161e4951ad5a9fd9c33b872f10fdd462c2d7db4f9e98e4ed4a0ed0fcac1c279a
 275.23 MB / 275.23 MB [==================================================] 2m7s
Copying config sha256:7954a6d6c52ef2879830fe61dfe4efdab634bb0062a11510b2e270b0a707ee5b
 415 B / 415 B [============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:161e4951ad5a9fd9c33b872f10fdd462c2d7db4f9e98e4ed4a0ed0fcac1c279a": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted

The user id on the PC where exporting was done is 500, while on importing PC it is 1000. Could it be a problem? Tested it, created the user with the same name and id and tried to import - the same error message.

@nerijus that bug got fixed recently upstream, could you try with a newer version of Podman?

Basically it is erroring out as it cannot create devices, but they are just ignored in recent versions of Podman
I downloaded source from git head (this repo), applied patches from PR #2028, but still get the same error when importing.
Custom made image, I can give a link if needed.

I pushed image to quay.io, and trying to pull on another PC errors out too:
$ podman pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10
Trying to pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10...Getting image source signatures

Copying blob sha256:a3ed95caeb02ffe68cdd9fd84406680ae93d633cb16422d00e8a7c22955b46d4

Copying blob sha256:d7243d1c911714be6577b92fe8b65e29b21ecae032d9908ffe9bb839c52d8938

 32 B / 32 B [==============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Failed
error pulling image "quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10": unable to pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10: unable to pull image: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:a02a4930cb5d36f3290eb84f4bfa30668ef2e9fe3a1fb73ec015fc58b9958b17": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted

@nerijus it seems I am not authorized to pull the image:

```
error pulling image "quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10": unable to pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
```

still unable to reproduce here, I have tried different combinations of `--storage-driver` and pull +  export/import.  Just to be sure, is the podman doing the pull also built from git?

Could you run with `--log-level debug`?

Yes, also from git. $ podman pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10 --log-level debug
flag provided but not defined: -log-level

Sorry.
$ podman --log-level debug pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10

DEBU[0119] Error pulling image ref //quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10:latest: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:a02a4930cb5d36f3290eb84f4bfa30668ef2e9fe3a1fb73ec015fc58b9958b17": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted 
Failed
ERRO[0119] error pulling image "quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10": unable to pull quay.io/nerijus/teamviewer10: unable to pull image: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:a02a4930cb5d36f3290eb84f4bfa30668ef2e9fe3a1fb73ec015fc58b9958b17": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
we don't really support F27 as it is EOL since 2018-11-30. Could you try on a newer system if it is still broken?  I've tried on two different F29 systems, going to try F28 now
Tried on kernel 4.19.10, and it worked.
Ok, I guess we can close this then.
